scouting_report: At around 6-foot-9 and 230 pounds, Watford looks to score inside and out. Will also pound the offensive glass. Has good skill, understanding of the game and desire. A natural scorer at the forward position, he is not shy on the offensive end. Should be one of the more productive offensive players in this class. Not the most explosive athlete. Could be limited on the defensive end, and improvement there will help dictate his future. Like a second-round choice in the NBA draft.

Average size for a combo forward. Not an overwhelming athlete, but has a skilled game. Comfortable with the ball but is primarily a scorer. Not known as a playmaker. Dangerous as a mid-range scorer off the dribble, post up and curl cuts. Has promise as a long-range shooter. Rebounds well but average defender at best. Bubble NBA prospect.

A wiry athlete with a tremendous wingspan. Utilizes both to make plays. Most effective as a multi positional defender who creates steals. Also is a quality rebounder. Limited offensively. Primarily a finisher around the basket off rebounds, dump-off passes and a straight-line drive. Not a good jump shooter at this point. Potential is there with his athleticism and length.

Average physical stature for a shooting guard. Has good body control and can be slippery on the drive but isn't an explosive athlete. Known as a scorer who can hit the three and also be effective off the dribble. Solid ball handler and passer for a shooting guard. Will rebound his position. Needs to get more physical but can guard multiple position. Should be a solid starter in college.

At 6-foot with a +6 wingspan, he has good size and length. Well-built and strong for age. Best offensively looking for teammates; sees the court well and protects ball. When looking to score, most efficient attacking basket. Lefty can hit shots but can improve from both midrange and three. Tough, hard-nosed point guard with high level competitive spirit. Plus defender due to his length, toughness, lateral quickness and feel. Will be able to guard most ones and plenty of twos at college level. Toughness and intangibles give him high floor in college. Development of jumper will be key in reaching and carving out a role in NBA.

Right around 6-foot-6 with long arms. Is a tremendous quick-twitch player who fits the mold of a classic wing at the NBA level. Is a very underrated passer, and knows how to make plays for teammates. Can really defend, and is a tremendous rebounder on both offense and defense. One of the hardest-playing kids in the class. Needs to continue to improve as an outside shooter, as he doesn’t project to be an impact scorer going forward. Projects as a first-round NBA draft pick.

One of the top combo forwards in the 2019 class. As an athlete has traits of a NBA player. Good strength, quality height for position and tremendous leaping ability. Not known for consistency of production, however. But has had flashes of brilliance. More of a finisher than a creator. Overall skill level could use improvement, but there is great upside due to his athleticism. Expectations to continue to develop skill-wise make him a potential lottery pick. Regardless, first-rounder simply based on athleticism.

Anthony is an explosive guard, a dynamic scorer and a rare two-way player who competes on both ends. He has an extremely quick first step, a burst of speed when he gets into the open court and athletically he’s gifted. A relentless driver who stays in attack mode, Anthony is special in transition and a very good finisher at the rim. He has also improved a great deal as a shooter from long-range. Anthony is comfortable shooting on the move, creating off the bounce or pulling up from mid-range. Defensively, Anthony gives terrific energy, slides his feet well laterally and has a great motor. Among the areas for growth are cutting down on turnovers, blending in more facilitating and tightening up his shot selection. Anthony is tracking as a future lottery pick and a player that will stick in the league for a long time.

A wiry athlete with bounce and burst. Average height at 6-4.5 for a shooting guard, but thin and in need of mass and strength gain. Tremendous finisher and a quality outside shooter. Not known for his playmaking ability, however. Rebounds his position well and has great potential as a defender. Fits mold of a three and D player and projects as a first-rounder.

Stands right around 6-foot-9, but doesn’t have great length. An extremely skilled forward, he shines when asked to stretch the floor and create some off the dribble. One of the best shooters in the class. Has the skill level and ability to put pressure on the defense out to 24 feet. Also can score from the mid-post, and is a good passer. Needs to get a lot stronger, and must continue to work on having a consistent motor. One of the tougher matchups in the class.

A thick, burly prospect, Carey is a low-post presence and strong rebounder with offensive potential. Carey has nice hands and very good touch around the basket. He has back-to-the basket moves and favors getting to a hook shot, but he can also bully his way to the rim. He has added a jump shot that extends out to the college 3-point stripe, which is key for his long-term potential. Carey does a very good job rebounding his area, pursues the ball off the rim and boxes out well. He has fine size for the position, but just an OK wingspan (7-foot-1) and standing reach (9’0.5”), by NBA standards. For Carey to maximize his potential, he’s going to have to monitor staying in shape, continue to expand the range on his jump shot and improve his lateral mobility. He is tracking as a first-round talent.

Has legit NBA center height with a frame that can fill out. A mobile athlete but not a powerful athlete. Has touch as a finisher and potential to be a space the floor five man. A solid passer from high post. Can block shots but needs to get tougher and more physical. Should develop into and NBA player.

Tough to find more solid and reliable combo forward in the 2019 class. Has strength, decent athleticism and is effective in traffic. Not a consistent long range shooter at this point, but extremely reliable in the midrange. High basketball IQ and great instincts. Projects as an adequate rebounder and defender at the next levels. Projects as a late first-rounder.

Solid build that helps him control space in the paint. Just average length for a post player. Decent athleticism. Great hands and good feet. Can score with back to basket. Has potential as a shooter. Comfortable with the basketball on perimeter. Rebounds well. Positional defender. Should develop into an NBA player.

A big, strong and burly post player, Tshiebwe is arguably the hardest playing prospect in the 2019 class. He’s tough, physical and doesn’t take plays off. Tshiebwe’s biggest value is in his rebounding, as he consistently goes to the glass, pursuing the ball with both hands. His motor seems to be endless and his brute strength is tough for opposing players to deal with at this stage. Offensively, Tshiebwe is quite raw. He looks to dunk everything. Next step is developing a go-to post move and improving his touch. He does consistent rim run, often beating the opposition for easy buckets. There’s value in his defense, because he doesn’t’ take plays off and most can’t handle the physical brand of basketball he plays. He also slides his feet relatively well and can handle switches on to wings on the perimeter. Tshiebwe will be a very productive college player, but at 6’8” there’s some size limitations. Tshiebwe should get drafted, but if he is going to stick in the league it will depend on his development.

Wiseman is 7-feet tall with a 7-6 wingspan. His standing reach of 9-3.5 is respectable, although not close to NBA centers like Rudy Gobert, Mohamed Bamba and JaVale McGee. Wiseman excels running the floor, moves fluidly and is an impressive all-around athlete. He consistently beats his man to the other end for easy baskets. He can go over his left shoulder for jump hooks, but also has developed his jump shot, especially from mid-range. Defensively, Wiseman slides his feet well. He is smart and has good instincts. He should be a better rebounder and shot blocker, although he’s not bad in either area. Developing his off hand for hook shots is another area for improvement. Wiseman is currently tracking as a lottery pick.

He needs to get into ideal shape and continue to work hard on refining his body because he is a naturally big kid. Before suffering a knee injury before the start of his junior season, he was tracking as one of the best point guards in the class. Is extremely tough, he has a good feel for the game, and loves to attack downhill off the dribble. Also won't play as a senior following another surgery. How he recovers from two years off is tough to know. Still, if healthy, he is a physical point guard who can make an open shot and is a good passer. The ability is there for him to be very good if he is 100 percent healthy in college.

Decent size for post player and has long arms. Plus athlete both vertically and laterally. One of top defenders in 2019 class regardless of position. High level shot blocker who uses length and timing to protect rim. Versatile defender who should be able to guard fours and fives. Improving offensive game, and has ability to post up and score from midrange. Continued development on offense and expanded range with jumper will be key for hitting longterm potential.

Hybrid forward with decent size at 6-7 and a +3 wingspan. High intangibles prospect who will be able to play the three and four at both sides of the court. Competitive prospect who plays hard every time out and consistently produces. Potent slasher from high post who gets to the line. Decent shooter from midrange; could improve from perimeter. Rebounds very well for position. Has size, athleticism and toughness to guard multiple positions. Improving handle and 3-point shot will be keys. Projects as multi-year starter for winning team at high major level.

Stocky combo guard who is slightly undersized but makes up for it with long arms. Thick, strong frame at this stage. Can play on and off ball but is most comfortable as a scorer. Range to three as shooter, has floater and uses strength to finish with contact. Handle and vision allow him to play on ball even if he’s shoot-first. Length and toughness make him strong on ball defender; should be able to guard one and twos. Just average athletically and somewhat in between positions, but has value at high major level and could ultimately be starter in Pac-12.

Has a slim frame but very quick with the basketball. Still developing physically. Gets to the rim and can finish with either hand. Shoots it well from deep. Good passer off the dribble. Can pressure a point guard defensively. Needs to improve his physical strength. Should develop into a Power 5 starter.

Scoring specialist with decent size and length. Has ability to play some backup one but his mentality is to score and he’s at his best in attack mode. Has quick first step and can get to rim or knockdown midrange jumpers. Streaky shooter from perimeter but can hit threes in bunches. Plus athlete who has upside as defender but will need improved commitment on that end. Can be turnover prone and making better decisions while becoming more consistent from the perimeter will be important. Projects as high level scorer in college regardless.

There aren't many prospects in the 2019 class that are as physically gifted as Cassius Stanley. At 6-foot-5, Stanley has good size and length for the shooting guard position. Athletically, Stanley is elite, as he's a very impressive leaper that moves fluidly and well laterally. Stanley excels in transition and on the defensive end, where he has a chance to be a very good perimeter defender. Stanley's shot mechanics and form look good and he's at the least an average long range shot at this point and there's reason to believe he'll turn into a pretty good one with time and work. He could improve his ball handling and like most players his age get stronger, but there's a lot to like about Stanley as a prospect and the direction he's headed.

Scoring wing with good size and long arms. Shoot-first but much improved efficiency. Big time threat off the dribble as a shooter from both midrange and three. Can knock down threes in bunches from perimeter and also has a strong one- and two-dribble pull-up game. Can get to the basket but is most comfortable scoring with his jumper. Size, length and athleticism give him upside as versatile defender. Can improve shot selection and handle. Projects as productive high major wing with NBA upside due to his shot making ability at his size.

Mobley is an extremely skilled four man with long arms and a high motor. He's got a terrific feel and can play facing up or with his back to the basket. Mobley has impressive ball skills for the position and can easily take smaller defenders off the dribble, while he's also a competent shooter. Has range to three with jumper, can attack from high post and has impressive footwork/post up ability. Finishes well with both hands. Rebounds in and outside of area. Blocks shots and passes well for position. NBA upside is uncertain due to game becoming smaller but his trajectory suggests he’ll get there and carve out a role.

Average size as a wing. Quite athletic in space, but needs more strength to be explosive in traffic. Primarily a finisher who likes to get to the rim or shoot from midrange. Respectable shooter from beyond the arc. Average ball handler who isn't known as a playmaking distributor. Solid rebounder. Has potential to be a high-level defender. On-the-bubble NBA prospect who must improve as a long-range shooter to make it at that level.

Only around 6-foot-2 without an elite wingspan. Needs to continue to refine his point guard skills, as he is slightly undersized to play off the ball. A good athlete who is capable of getting into the lane. There aren’t many more gifted scorers. Right now his physical profile isn’t that of an NBA guard. Might be the type of player who is so good at his skill that he breaks the mold ends up making it to the league.

At 6-foot-4, uses size to get shots off over smaller defenders, A strong guard who makes his home off the ball and can really score. Uses his intelligence to go off the dribble and create for himself or others. Isn’t a jet with the basketball or super explosive athletically, but he does have the physical ability to excel on both ends of the floor. Ability to score translates to the highest level of college basketball.

An athletic and dynamic combo guard. Plays with strength while slightly undersized. One of the top perimeter players in the 2019 class. A three-level scorer with great explosiveness off the dribble. His pull-up jumper is his most dangerous weapon. Also shoots the ball well off the catch. A quality rebounder for a guard. An efficient ball handler and playmaker with an impressive assist-to-turnover ratio. A strong competitor on the defensive end. Finding a balance between distributing and scoring is key, although there is no glaring weakness to his game. A future first-round pick with natural development and progress.

At 6-foot-9 and 230 pounds, Jones is a fairly mobile center who runs the floor well. Should make an impact rebounding the basketball. Has good hands, and can carve space as a rebounder against taller players. Has to continue to work on his touch. Athletically he is just average, and because of that he isn’t a threat to protect the rim. Shows effort and should be a good position defender. Can be inconsistent as a finisher on the interior. Needs to continue to refine his offensive game, but there are solid measurables and a good motor that help him produce against high-level players.

A high-motor big man who absolutely dominates around the rim. One of the most productive players in the class of 2019. Could be the best overall rebounder. Offensive game continues to improve. Moves his feet pretty well as a defender, and shows some ability to block shots. However, he isn’t going to be someone who scares offensive players from driving into the lane. Projects as a first-round NBA draft choice.

A true wide body who controls space in the paint. Practically impossible to move out of position. Not the quickest, but does have quality footwork. Not a rim protector, but a positional defender. Much more effective defending the interior than on the perimeter. Finishes with touch inside, but has limited range. Productive rebounder with sure hands. Likely an NBA player but tough to project as a high draft pick.

Solid build and solid athleticism for a small forward. Can play power forward as well. Has a complete skill set but doesn't excel in any one particular area offensively. Primarily scores on drives into the lane. Must respect his 3-point range. Conscientious defender and rebounder.

At 6-foot-5, Okoro has a chiseled frame, fairly good size for a perimeter prospect and a +3.5 wingspan. Okoro is a good athlete who plays with toughness. He’s effective in transition and playing a slasher type role. Okoro has a lot of potential on the defensive end, where he can guard both perimeter positions and - in a pinch - small post players. Okoro is also a good rebounder. His energy is contagious. The next step is fine tuning his offensive game. He’ll need to improve his ballhandling and long-range shooter. Okoro projects as a multi-year college player, but with improved offensive skill could be a drafted prospect.

This thick, burly post player has quite a bit of potential on the offensive end. Bacot has a thick build. He has a +3 wingspan, but is just an OK athlete, which hinders his long-term potential. Bacot has very good hands and nice touch around the goal. He’s also able to step away and make mid-range jump shots. In time, his range should extend further. Bacot has good footwork on the block and finishes off plays well with his right hand. Bacot has gotten stronger over the course of his career and he’s developed into a very good area rebounder. He goes after the ball with both hands and fights for position inside. Ultimately, Bacot projects as a very good, productive college player.

Fine size (6’4”), average length and impressive vertical leaping ability. He built his calling card early in his career as an athletic scorer. Has good shot mechanics and there’s potential as a shooter, although his percentages don’t reflect that. An OK ball handler, but more of a straight-line driver at this stage. Potential as a defender, as he does possess fine quickness/speed. Long-term areas for improvement include consistency as a shooter, ability to break defenders down off the dribble and efficiency.

Ideal size for combo guard prospect at 6-foot-5. He has a sturdy build, good length and explosive athleticism. All the physical gifts are there. An aggressive scorer that operates well in ball screen situations, he’s able to break down defenders off the bounce and is a very good finisher at the rim. He’s also a good passer with impressive vision. Has become a good shooter from distance (45-percent from 3 in his final summer). He has good shot mechanics, is a good shooter on the catch or move, and is also equipped to pull-up rom mid-range and make shots. Defensively has all the tools, but it’s an area for improvement because he often times loses focus. Staying dialed in at all times and his shot selection are other areas for improvement. Edwards is most effective when he has the ball in his hands, and that’s why we like the comparison to James Harden. Edwards is tracking as the potential No. 1 overall pick in the 2020 NBA Draft.

A big wing (6-9) who combines good size and length with very nice athleticism. Versatility is also a strength. It is possible he spends some time as a shooting guard, and also could slide all the way down and be a stretch power forward. Has ability to naturally get buckets in the mid-range. Needs to continue to get stronger and work on his perimeter jumper. Should develop into a first-round NBA selection.

Average size and length but makes up for it almost everywhere else. Highly skilled point guard with high basketball IQ. Good decision maker who limits turnovers. Does nice job balancing scoring and creating. Very good ball handler with plus vision. Plus shooter who can score from all three levels. Overachieves on defense due to motor and feel. More point guard quick than athletic; how he improves athletically as he matures will be something to watch. Projects as multi-year starter on winning team at high major level.

Undersized at 5-8 but strong, quick and competitive. A great lefty set up man. High assist numbers and low turnover rate. Three-point shot must be respected. Loves to pressure ball full court. Size can be issue in half court on defense but quick, strong hands and fights for position. Can lead a team.

Strong, sturdy frame. Good size for position. More combo guard than true point guard. Has good vision but tends to be shoot-first. Decent athlete but changes speeds pretty well. Best shooting from the perimeter. Ability to score and pass makes him best in transition. Has tools to be respectable defender. Rebounds well for position. Improving efficiency as shooter/scorer while cutting down on turnovers will be important. Projects as four-year college player.

At 6-foot-8 with a huge wingspan, his specialty is blocking shots. He is one of the better rim protectors in the class, and shows good timing and instinct to block shots both from the weakside and on the ball. Offensively he is extremely raw, and he has to improve both his hands and his touch. Runs the court and gives good energy on the offensive glass. Holds his won when rebounding, though needs to continue to get stronger. He will have to improve on offense to be a consistent contributor in college.

Undersized for the position at 5-11, but has impressive end-to-end speed and good athleticism. He’s at best when he can push the tempo and get out in transition. Is a capable scorer and tough to contain off the bounce. He’s aggressive, hardnosed and - when he wants to be - a pesky on-ball defender. The biggest areas for concern are playing under control, shot selection and consistency as a shooter.

In the mold of a quintessential high-level NBA scoring wing. Quality height at 6-6 with strength and explosive athleticism. Proficient scorer at all three levels. Effective in transition and in the half court as a scorer. Not a distributor and prone to turn the ball over excessively. Rebounds position well and has potential as a defender. Lottery pick-type potential.

Decent length for a small forward. Solid athlete who plays with great energy and grinds. Scores off the dribble. Finishes well with left hand. Has potential as 3-point shooter. Passes the ball well and can handle. Dangerous rebounder. Solid all around player. Doesn't overwhelm in any one area.

Josiah James is a strong, physical combo guard. He has a strong, chiseled frame with wide shoulders and good length. James is a good all-around athlete, although has just average speed. He’s a physical driver that looks to attack, but does so with his head up looking to create for others. James plays at a good pace and does a nice job of changing speeds. He is a fine finisher at the rim, and his strength comes into play here. The southpaw is comfortable pulling up for mid-range jump shots, but his long-range shot is the area he’ll need to improve the most. On defense, James can defend multiple positions and there’s significant upside on this end. To go with long-range shooting, other areas for growth include ball handling, valuing the ball more and shooting on the move. James projects as a potential late first-round draft pick, although there’s good upside here if he continues to develop.

There might not be a greater physical specimen in the 2019 class. Is right around 6-foot-10 and 235 pounds, and looks to be cut from granite. Combination of size and strength makes him one of the more imposing players in this class. Has good hands and is a capable finisher around the rim. To this point, production hasn’t matched his potential. Needs to become a good rebounder, and right now that is a weakness. If he can find a way to translate physical ability into basketball production, he could end up being one of the toughest matchups on the low block in this class.

Big build. Controls his space. Average length for a post player. Great hands and has solid footwork. Not overly athletic. Composed with the basketball. Effective in high-low game. Rebounds position. Positional defender. Not a shot blocker. Should be a contributor on the college level.

Undersized for shooting guard. Plays with strength but average athleticism. Deadly shooter. Put up prodigious scoring numbers in high school but modest numbers on the Nike EYBL circuit. Good ballhandler. More of a set-up guy than a penetrator. Needs to work on defense and rebounding. Will knock down shots in college.

A well-built and physical athlete. Capable of dominating defensively at either guard position. Highly competitive and will chase down the ball. Loves to attack the basket and also shoots it well from outside. Solid as a handler and passer. Has the makings of a non-flashy but substantive NBA prospect.

Not especially big or explosive. Is a little bit longer than his brother P.J. - who also played at Purdue. Isaiah is a little better with the dribble, though. Smart player and has a pedigree. Will likely more than make up for his lack of size and athleticism. Defense could be a problem going forward. Makes a quality impression because of his ability to make shots and his basketball IQ.

A big perimeter prospect that possesses shooting ability and skill. At 6-8, he has very good size for the position and he's a good athlete. He has a solid build and one that would fill out further in a college weight room. He already plays well through contact. Shooting is among his biggest strengths. He has good shot mechanics, shot preparation and good range. He's also a good passer. Areas for improvement include creating space for pull-ups and ball handling. Wilson has the chance to be a first-round draft pick with development.

Decent size for a post player, but not overly athletic. Has a tremendous feel for the game. Plays with great energy and competes at a high level. Highly skilled inside 15 feet of the basket. Has an array of moves to form a complex offensive game around the basket. Not much of a scoring threat on the perimeter, but comfortable with the ball and has a positive assist-to-turnover ratio. Good positional defender but not known as a shot blocker. Projects as a second round pick.

Undersized at around 6-foot-6, for a power forward. Athleticism and elite motor more than make up for physical shortcomings. No questioning his toughness or ability to produce. Has shown signs of improving perimeter jumper. On defense he gives everything he has. Is someone who will rebound the ball and even block a few shots. Because of his motor, plays taller than he is. If he can develop a consistent jumper he could even morph into a P.J. Tucker-type of player.

He has long arms, and is a good athlete but toughness and motor are the biggest parts of his game. Is an intriguing player because at 6-foot-7 he plays inside mostly, but also can score some from the perimeter. Williams can hit the mid-range jumper, but he is at his best driving from the mid-range to the lane. Is an excellent rebounder. He needs to continue to improve his skill game on the outside, but his strength and athleticism should make him a tough matchup in college.

Nice length for a combo forward. Extremely thin. Runs the floor well. Good athlete but struggles in traffic. Plays above the rim when jumping off one foot in transition. Has touch as a shooter. Prefers to score off the dribble. Has a good feel for the game as a ballhandler and passer. Must get stronger to be effective defensively. Has a lot of upside if he develops his body.

Has the size and frame of a developing NBA power forward. Has a high ceiling for further physical development. A bouncy and active player as a defender and rebounder. Quality shot blocker. Defense is ahead of his offense. Not a primary scoring threat nor a threat as a passer. Development in college will be critical to NBA prospects. Projects as a second round pick unless he makes a big leap offensively in college.

Average size for a point guard. Solid athlete. Not overly explosive athletically, but plays strong in traffic and plays through contact. Loves to pressure the ball defensively. Gets to the rim off the dribble and at times can overly force the issue. Competent shooter on pull ups and off the catch. A willing passer who can find teammates and create opportunities. Aggressive and competitive player.

Big-bodied shot maker. More efficient from three than inside the arc. Not an explosive athlete but gathers well for jump shot. Not a get-to-the rim guy. Solid all around skills. Good defender with knack for steals defensively. Holds his own as a rebounder.

Has fine size for the position with average length and athletic ability. Is a forward prospect that possess a fairly good skill set for a face up four-man. Fairly tough and scrappy. Has good shot mechanics and has potential as a shot maker from distance. He’ll also post up from time to time. Has good hands, touch and can get to a jump hook. Areas for improvement include strength, athleticism and ball handling.

A long, lean and versatile forward prospect. He’s active, energetic and consistently plays hard. He’s fluid, runs the floor with ease and possesses good athleticism. Has value as a rim protector. Has good timing to go with the length, athleticism and instincts. Offensively can stretch the floor with his jump shot and is capable of heating up from distance. He’s also a good shooter from mid-range. Especially considering his size, he handles the ball well and is able to start the break off rebounds. Areas for improvement include strength and developing a back-to-the-basket game. He improved as much as any player in the class over his last year and a half, and if that continues he’s tracking as a potential first-rounder one day.

An alert player with instincts and top-notch athleticism. Has solid size for a small forward but does need to gain strength. High energy player who can both start the fast break and finish it. Solid all-around skill game but has room to improve weak hand. Shooting is developing and has potential. Very active defender who is a terrific shot blocker at 6-8. He as the athleticism and upside to be a potential lottery pick in time.

A versatile perimeter prospect with impressive size, length and athleticism. Can be utilized off the ball as a two-guard, but also has combo guard qualities. Has good vision and is a quality passer. An improving shooter, especially in catch-and-shoot situations. Slides his feet well on defense and his length factors in. He’s at best defending twos and threes. The next steps in his development are improving his ball handling, consistency as a long-range shooter and competing every possession. With development, Johnson would be a NBA Draft pick.

At only about 6-foot-1, is an off-the-ball player who has the potential to be a high-level scorer. Built like a tailback, is fast, explosive and powerful. A good shooter from deep, and should only get better with reps. When attacking the rim he uses his significant vertical explosion and strength to finish through taller players. On the defensive end his toughness and athleticism give him significant potential, though he does need to have more of a commitment - especially off the ball. A solid prospect who can play both guard spots, but is more suited as a scorer who is a secondary ball handler and decision maker.

Good size for a college point guard with continuously improving athleticism. Mixes scoring and creating very well. Extremely skilled point guard with high basketball IQ. High level handle to go with quick first step help him create space and get in lane. Consistent 3-point shooter, has midrange jumper and floater packages, attacks basket and not afraid of contact. Very good vision and feel; consistently makes right play. Hard worker who has progressed throughout high school career. Will need to get stronger in order to hold own on defense at college level. Has NBA upside but floor is ideal high major starting point guard at college level.

At 6-foot-3 he can play either on or off the ball, but he prefers to be a scorer first and a passer second. Should bring toughness and leadership to Nebraska. He is a good athlete, but not someone who pops on tape in that regard. On defense he is a competitor and should be able to defend multiple positions. He has never been asked to carry the load offensively, so he is comfortable as a role player - and should excel in that spot at the college level.

Average athlete at best. Has a presence skill wise and not because of physicality. Highly skilled player who can handle and knockdown a shot. Defending and rebounding will present a challenge. Smart, skilled player who can play a shooter and secondary handler role.

Has a thin, slender build. He’s fairly long, has a quick first step and is a good overall athlete. Impresses with raw speed and physical gifts at the combo guard spot. Has a burst of speed when he gets into the open court and is at his best when the game is going up and down or he’s attacking the rim. Because of his length and lateral quickness, Anderson projects as a good on-ball defender. Shooting is an area for improvement, as he’s streaky at best at this stage. He’ll also need to get stronger and continue to improve as a playmaker.

Solid height and good frame for a combo forward. Reliable long distant shooter with a high ceiling in that area. Can score off dribble as well. Solid defender and rebounder. Quality shot blocker. Not an overly explosive athlete but a good athlete with awareness, timing and anticipation. Not much off a playmaker as a passer. Should develop into an NBA player.

A jumbo wing, has impressive size to go with wide shoulders and long arms. Has the tools to be a good perimeter defender, but has the size and length to potential guard PF’s in college. He’s fluid, mobile and a solid all-around athlete. At this stage, he’s a capable shooter off the catch from distance, although a tad streaky. He’s a good passer with impressive vision. The next steps in his development are tightening his handle and consistently playing hard on both ends. Should end up being an NBA draft choice.

Extremely athletic and versatile wing; should be able to play the two and the three at the next level on both sides of court. Talented slasher due to high level body control and quick first step. Much improved shooter; now has legitimate range to three, even if he’s more lethal as slasher. Elite in transition due to motor and physical tools. Plus rebounder for wing; uses his athleticism and motor to grab boards in and out of area. Very high feel; passes ball well and reads opponents well on defense. Elite athleticism gives him significant upside. Continued progress as shooter while improving ball skills against pressure will determine hitting potential. Lottery pick upside if skill can continue to catch up to athletic gifts.

Mann is a slender, lanky point guard prospect loaded with potential. Mann has average size for the position by NBA standards. He’s quick, aggressive and handles the ball well. Although a primary ball handler, Mann does have a tendency to get into score mode. He’s a gifted scorer that can heat up in a hurry. He’s a good shooter from distance, and has range that extends well past the college 3-point stripe. Mann’s shooting numbers will go up further when he sures up his shot selection. He’s also effective from mid-range, where he can drop in floaters or pull-up for jumpers. Mann has fine vision and passing ability, too. Defensively, Mann has potential, as he’s quick and has good instincts, but he could be more consistent in this area. Other areas for improvement include taking better care of the ball and getting stronger. He is a first-round talent.

Baker has good size, a sturdy build and a strong base for a center prospect. He carves out good position in the paint, is a good area rebounder and has immense potential on the offensive end. Has good hands and nice touch around the goal. He is a confident shooter from mid- and long-range, and has value in this area. Has an impressive feel for the game and is a very good passer. He’s also a capable rim runner that plays and competes on both ends of the floor. Consistency can be an area for improvement, as well as continuing to develop his post counters. Baker is tracking as a multi-year college player.

A quick leaping, strong athlete. Controls space as a high motor athlete with pop. Will go get the basketball. Can score inside and is comfortable with the basketball on the perimeter. Has defensive versatility. Should develop into a starter with time.

A combo forward who brings good athleticism and a solid dose of toughness, Should find a role at the high-major level. Has a strong body that will really fill out in a college weight program, and then his skill continues to get better. Right now he is at his best attacking off the dribble and getting into the lane, but his outside shot has shown signs of improvement. On the defensive end he should be someone who can guard multiple positions, and provide significantly versatility.

Has good size for a wing scorer. Physically strong with a great shooting stroke. Only needs a small window to get his shot off. Very good shooter off catch from three and midrange. A straight-line driver with a quality pull-up jumper. More of a scorer than a playmaker for others. Strong and composed with the ball though. Strong rebounder and solid defender. Has the makings of a Three and D NBA player.

Doesn’t stand out physically at around 6-foot-1 with an average wingspan. Is a good shooter from deep, and has shown improved athleticism. On defense, competes and has solid size for the position. One of the smartest players in the class. Has shown ability to compete against the best and make others better. His intangibles and ability to make plays for others separate him as one of the best floor generals in the class of 2019. Is a borderline first-round NBA draft choice.

Has solid size for a combo guard to go along with very good athleticism. Quality scorer off the dribble and can get to the rim. Good handler and passer - especially as a secondary ball handler. Improving outside shooter. Projects as a valuable multi positional defender. Needs to continue to develop physically.

Not a great athlete, and doesn’t have prototype size for an off guard. Tough player who can really score. Good going to the rim and shooting from the mid-range. Long-range jumper continues to improve. On defense he is committed and is extremely tough. Has a good motor, and gets the most out of his physical tools. Should develop into a starter for the Cavaliers.

The son of former NBA player Dale Davis. Is a hair under 6-foot-9 and is currently limited to playing center. A quickly improving interior player who runs the floor well, has great hands, and can rebound the ball. Needs to continue to improve on his range and adapt to the new age of basketball. His rebounding, athleticism and interior scoring should allow him to play at the highest level of basketball. Expect him to be a future first-round pick.

Need to gain strength, especially in the lower body. A long presence around the rim. Runs court well and has decent reactions in half court. Average skill set and hands for a rim protecting center. Will pursue the basketball. Has potential skill-wise. Has solid floor as a prospect, but projects as an NBA rebounder and defender.

At 6-4 and 195 pounds has the size to play any perimeter position. Is a scoring combo guard with the ability to make others better. Ball handling allows him to play on or off the ball. Has to continue to improve as an outside jump shooter, but the tools are there for him to be a high-level scorer at the college level. In order to go from good college player to NBA prospect, will need to become a more consistent shooter. If he does that, he should get a great shot to make a roster.

Nnaji has good size for the position at 6-11, but his wingspan is just OK at 7-1. He’s mobile, runs the floor with ease and consistently rim runs. A rapidly developing post player, Nnaji brings value in his ability to make mid-range jump shots, finish off plays around the basket and with his rebounding. Nnaji has good hands, nice touch and is a fine finisher around the goal. Nnaji is a very good shooter from mid-range and in time his range will extend further. Nnjai does a good job of rebounding on the glass and competes on defense. He’s just OK as a rim protector, but gives good effort.

Extremely versatile defender with decent size and length. Should easily be able to guard three and four at next level. Somewhat in-between positions on offense but gifted athlete that can be used in variety of ways. Quick off feet and good leaper. Rebounds the ball very well on both ends. Gets out in transition and finishes around the basket. Needs to improve as shooter and ball handler, while consistency has been an issue. Projects as ideal multi-year role player on winning team at college level.

Has solid size and athleticism for a small forward. Plays with strength. Slasher who prefers to score off the dribble. Reluctant shooter off the catch beyond the arc at this point. Not really a playmaker for teammates but is a solid ball handler. Plays with s good motor. Strong rebounder who is also active on defense.

A good athlete on the wing with solid length and versatility. Has improved greatly throughout his high school career. He is at his best as a shot maker from deep. Given his length and athleticism, his ability to shoot makes him dangerous on the wing when surrounded by drivers. Braun can also attack a closeout, and is getting better at finishing through contact. While he might not start right away, Braun should develop into a quality contributor in college.

A long, lean wing prospect with good size for the position at 6-foot-6. A good all-around athlete that will be able to guard threes and twos at the next level. His shot mechanics are good and he's a good shooter off the catch, particularly from mid-range. From deep he's inconsistent, but we project that he'll be good shooter from distance in time. Is a good rebounder for a perimeter player. Areas for improvement include strength, consistency from long-range and ball handling. There's a lot of upside here and could see him developing into a NBA player after college.

A smooth, body control type athlete. Not overly physical or explosive. Great instincts for scoring and coming up with the ball. Great shooting stroke from deep and at the line. More shooting guard than point guard at 6-2. Will need to focus on defense in college. Should be a starter before his career is finished.

An athletic and long big man who can run the floor. Stands a little over 6-foot-8 and knows how to block shots as well as rebound. Gets most of his buckets by outrunning defenders and catching dump offs, but he is improving facing the rim. His footwork is solid on the low block. Because of his frame and solid length, has upside left in his game. Is someone who brings a tremendous motor to the court every time he steps on the floor.

Lefty has very slender frame but has some athletic pop. Decent length for position. Quick to the basketball and the rim. More finisher than playmaker. Shot needs work. Multi-positional defender. Must get stronger but has promising potential.

A thick, burly center prospect. A below-the-rim player with a fairly impressive skill set. Mitchell has very good hands and nice touch around the basket. He can score in the post with hook shots over either shoulder, and also is capable of stretching the floor with his jump shot from mid-range out to three. Mitchell needs to get in better shape and at this stage struggles scoring over size and length. He’s tracking as a productive four-year player.

Shooting specialist with decent size and average length. Very good shooter who can knock down jumpers off catch and pull from both midrange and three. Specializes as shooter but also sees the court well and is a good passer. Plays hard and respectable competitor on both ends. Somewhat in-between positions on defense; may end up most apt to guarding fours at the high major level. Improving handle and defense will go a long way in hitting potential. Shooting ability should help him carve out role in Pac-12.

Score-first point guard who has flamboyant and confident approach to game. Showman who can use his handle to create space for himself or take opponents off the dribble and get to the rim or find open teammates. Has range to three with jumper and is a streak shooter but can hit jumpers in bunches. Shoot-first but does have vision to create for others. Plays hard on defense and has plus lateral quickness. Needs to get stronger to finish with contact better and hit his upside on defense. Has high major starter upside.

Elite size for position. Huge size makes him a plus rim protector. Not athletic but moves well enough considering size. Soft hands and good touch around basket. Rebounds area well and does nice job finishing on the offensive glass. Despite size and skill, little precedent for players his size in recent years having success is concerning. If mobility improves, could really overachieve. Worst case is backup center at high major level; improved strength and proven durability could make him much better than that.

Very good size for small forward. Has long arms. Should be able to play both three and four at college level. Has shown nonstop progress throughout high school career. Extremely versatile player who is good – albeit not great yet – at most areas. Fluid athlete with impressive body control. Improving shooter with range to three, although still not consistent. Has upside as a rebounder and multi-position defender. Needs to get stronger, improve his handle and become more a consistent shooter/scorer. Has first-round pick upside.

Baby-faced four man at 6-11 - and potentially still growing - with 6-10 wingspan. Plus athlete with terrific feel for the game for young player at position. Very good instincts on defense, from rebounding ability to feel as defender. Can grab a board and start break. Has terrific body control and can play some three due to first step and long strides. Competent shooter out to three, can also hit pull-up jumpers and floaters from midrange. Also comfortable with back to the basket and attacking offensive glass. Gaining strength will be key moving forward, also can improve jumper and handle. Has narrow frame so being able to put on weight will be key. Has lottery pick upside with added strength, skill.

Slim build with decent length for a shooting guard. A smooth athlete with good body control. Sees the court and has a good handle. Loves to go left and score with his right hand jump shot. Great mechanics coming off screens without the ball and gets his shot off quickly from three. A savvy rebounder who also gets steals. Needs to become more physical as a defender.

Big receiver with high-level body control. Has a basketball background and was a high recruit in hoops and it shows in how strong his hands are and how well his timing is in jump ball situations. Has a strong body and can box out smaller defensive backs or simply jump over them. Big catch radius and physical style of play allow him to create easy separation. Not a burner but runs well enough to make plays down the field and is tough to bring down in the open field. Can improve as a route runner and have never benefited from an off-season program since he’s always playing basketball in the off-season. Plans to double sport in college, at least initially and it will be interesting to see if he is able to stick with both sports throughout college. Projects as an impact upper tier Power 5 starter and future mid-late round NFL draft pick.

Has great size and is tough to move off his spot. Average athlete at best. Plays with high center of gravity and isn't an above-the-rim type player. Can score off back-to-the-basket post moves. Has good hands and corrals rebounds. A project/potential type big man. Should be a contributor who could become a starter with development.

Versatile wing prospect with good size and average length (6-7 wingspan). Plus athlete who is shifty and gets off his feet well in open court. Can play both two and three due to his size, feel and skill. Capable but streaky shooter. Playmaker off the bounce who can create or efficiently score with his midrange jumper. Excellent passer. Has upside as defender due to size and feel. Needs to keep adding strength, improving consistency from perimeter and improving handle. Has NBA upside but way too early to project him there; adjustment from international to USA game will be big.

Tough, defensive minded point guard with respectable floor game. Thrives on the defensive end due to his strength and toughness. Projects as a very good on-the-ball defender at high-major level. Best as a slasher offensively. Has improved as three-point shooter but can still get better there. Has playmaking ability off bounce but his mentality is to score first. Enters college very physically strong; development of jumper and creativity as passer will be important. At worst should be role player; defense and intangibles give him upside to be better.

At around 6-foot-5 with an impressive wingspan, he is a wing scorer who should be able to apply significant pressure to a defense. Best asset is his ability to shoot from deep. Has good range shooting both off the dribble and off the catch. Has length and toughness to defend multiple positions. Solid ball handler and can make decisions when attacking the rim. Needs to continue to get stronger, but offensively brings a lot to the table. Has potential to be a high-level shooter before his college career is finished.

At 6-foot-6, he brings a unique and impressive skillset to the table. Is a versatile wing who can also handle the ball. Intends to play point guard in college, though he is likely best served as a playmaker who is used off the ball to create on closeouts. What he does best is get to the rim where he can finish with athleticism and toughness through contact. He has to continue to refine his jumper and prove he is 100 percent healthy coming off of a leg injury. On the defensive end, he looks to be someone who can bring a lot of positional versatility and fit into almost any system.

Has average size for a point guard. Possesses quickness and slick ball handling. Can penetrate and make plays but greatest strength is shooting. Proficient from deep and in mid-range off the dribble and the catch. Capable of hounding the ball as an on-the-ball defender. Alert to snagging rebounds and quick to loose balls. All-around solid guard with NBA talent.

Hybrid forward who is pushing 6-10 with a 6-11 wingspan. Extremely versatile; can easily play both the three and four in college. Has very good basketball IQ and uses it through passing ability, rebounding and on defense. Has knack for reading defense and picking off errant passes. Sees court well and always makes right pass. Quality rebounder in and outside of area. Can score facing up or posting up with range to three. Can continue to improve his handle and efficiency shooting the ball, along with ability to create own shot off bounce. Above average but not elite athlete. Should be very good college player. NBA going smaller helps his stock; could find role as hybrid forward in league with continued development.

A strongly built shooting guard at 6-5. Strength and body control are his calling cards athletically. A high level shooter who also handles the ball well. Has deep range and can score in the midrange. Is a capable rebounder. Decent defender but not the quickest laterally. Projects to put up hefty scoring numbers in college with his shooting touch.

Good physique for a point guard, and he can also play shooting guard. A physical slasher who passes well off the dribble. Loves to set players up but is a quality scorer as well. Quality rebounder as a guard who can defend at a high level and defend multiple positions. Plays with an edge and competes.Could develop into an NBA player.

Nickname is “Turbo,” and that describes him perfectly. From baseline to baseline might be the fastest guard in the country. A jet with the basketball, and also is a very good vertical and lateral athlete. While he isn’t a pure point guard, he sees the floor well in transition and is a better passer than most expect. His shot is inconsistent right now, but he shows signs of being solid from beyond the arc. Has all the tools to be an impressive guard, but he needs to be more consistent with his decision making. Should develop into a starter for Auburn.

Gultekin is a promising combo forward with good physical tools and skill set. He has the size and length to play the three or four. While Gultekin will often settle for jumpers, he's a very capable shooter who can knock down shots from the perimeter and midrange. For his size, he’s a fine ball handler and every now and then will go to the post, where he likes to get to a left-handed hook. Areas for improvement include strength, consistency from deep as a shooter and his shot selection.

Has very good size but just has average length for a wing. A high-major perimeter prospect. Is an impressive athlete that gets off the ground with ease and quickness. Has good shot mechanics and a smooth, easy stroke. He’s also a good passer. The next step in his development is improving off the dribble, as he’s more of a straight-line driver at this stage. Also needs to get stronger.

Has good length, and stands around 6-foot-4. At times is able to play point guard, but is at his best off the ball. Good mid-range shooter, will be a good defender. Has the intangibles to help his team win. While the physical tools won’t blow anybody away, he should score at a solid clip. The type of leader every successful team needs. Not a super explosive athlete or an elite shooter. The type of kid who knows how to get things done.

Quality athlete with a blend of quickness, speed and leaping ability. Has the frame to take on more mass. In the process of increasing strength. Primarily a transition and midrange offensive player. Skill package is expanding and is known as an upside player. Versatile defensive player who is a quality rebounder.

Solid length for a wing. Athletic but slender. A skilled ball handler and passer. Has a feel for the game as a slasher. Great body control with the ball. Efficient finisher going to the basket. Long range shooting is a weakness. Tremendous potential as a multi-positional defender. Plays with energy and rebounds his position. Don't think he'll be drafted but could play his way on to an NBA roster.

Has great length, quick feet and good hands. A natural, fluid athlete. Weight gain and strength gain will likely determine ultimate ceiling. Skilled around the basket. Shows promise skill wise away from the basket. Solid shooter to 17-feet. Active rebounder. Finishes with touch in the lane. Lack of strength is a weakness. Will likely be a late first-round or early second-round selection in the NBA draft.

Good size, very long arms. Elite rim protector who uses size, length and timing to block and contest shots. Rebounds area well. Raw as scorer but has shown ability to hit midrange jumpers and has budding hook shot. Very good passer for position. Competitive kid who plays hard. Physically immature at this stage, and adding strength will be big for production on both sides of court. Has NBA upside if offense can catch up to defense, but that will take time.

A quick athlete with average size, at best. Quick with the basketball, but not a leaper at the rim. Scoring-oriented guard with a dangerous pull-up jumper who can make a slick pass. A good lefty shooter with some range. Needs to get stronger and more physical on the defensive end.

Quite slender with a narrow frame as 6-8 interior player. Greatest strength is his bouncy athleticism. Has quick feet and is extremely quick off the floor. Plays with a high motor. Has good hands. Finishes well 15-feet and in. Can score off a couple bounces. Decent overall skill level. Shows promise to improve skill wise. Talented shot blocker and quick to the ball off the boards. Ability to put on weight is a big question. Needs to extend shooting range.

Good strength and build for a combo forward. Not an overwhelming explosive athlete, but has effective athleticism because of strength. Rebounds well and is strong with the ball. Not a go-to scorer but comes up with buckets with hustle and in transition. Respectable outside shooter. Can guard the three and the four. Should start alongside his brother Kalib - at some point - for the Cowboys.

Has a strong build and is effective in traffic. Not an explosive athlete but fluid with the basketball. Has great hands. Can stretch the defense well beyond the 3-point line. Handles and passes well. Has a nice pull-up jumper. Limited foot speed but high basketball IQ. Should develop into a Power 5 starter.

A long, lean left-handed forward. A very good leaper and a fluid athlete that bounces off the ground. At this stage he’s at best in transition and playing the role of a slasher. He also has the tools to be a good, multi-positional defender. One area for improvement is his jump shot, as he lacks consistency and has a low release point on his shot. Tracking as a good four-year college player with upside because of his physical gifts.

Saw his stock really soar after doing a post-graduate year. He has very good athleticism and is a powerfully built prospect. He is at his best at the power forward spot, but he does have the ability to switch ball screens, so he can play some on the wing. Jackson is a developing shooter from deep, and is at his best going to the rim and finishing with power and athleticism. He is a solid rebounder, though that should improve as he gets stronger and pays more attention to detail in a college program.

A slender power forward prospect. Is intriguing because of his size, mobility and tools on offense. Has OK length and athleticism, but he plays fairly hard and competes on both ends. His lack of strength is a hinderance at this stage, but getting stronger should come once he’s in a college weight room. Has a nice set of mitts, good touch around the basket and possesses good shooting ability. He has good shot mechanics and range on his shot. Outside of strength, other areas for improvement include his back-to-the-basket game and consistency as a rebounder.

At 6-foot-9 and around 225 pounds, is a tough interior player. He doesn’t have the longest wingspan, but makes up for that by with an extremely quick second jump which allows him to rebound well. Finishes around the rim. His touch right now is consistent out to 15 feet, and flashes the ability to shoot from distance. That said, it is his toughness and motor that make him a potentially excellent college basketball player. He is a player who will be a double-double threat during his time in college.

A strong, tough and physical post player. His biggest value lies in his ability to rebound and protect the rim. Although a little undersized, Iwaukor is a very good athlete that is quick off his feet, which helps negate being slightly undersized. Plays with tremendous energy and competes. Offensively, Iwaukor still has some developing to do. He looks to dunk everything around the rim, but doesn’t have a developed go-to move and has just OK touch. Iwaukor is a four-year college player.

A bigger point guard at 6-foot-2, he prefers to score over distribute. Has the ability to make shots or get into the lane, though is at his best attacking the rim. While his first instinct is to put the ball in the basket, Buie can also see the floor fairly well and is a solid passer. On the defensive end he possesses nice potential because of his plus wingspan and pretty good lateral movement. Has the versatility to defend either spot in the backcourt. Is the type of solid prospect who tends to overachieve during his college career. Should be a starter at some point in his career.

Decent size for a shooting guard. Not an explosive athlete, but a smooth player with great body control. A tremendous shooter with an effortless release. Can score off the dribble or the catch. Needs only a small window from defender to score. Needs to get stronger. Solid all-around skill set but niche is scoring. Average defender and rebounder. Should be a multi-year starter in college.

Late blooming shooting guard with good size and length. Very good athlete who uses his physical tools on both ends. Plus shooter who can knock down shots off the bounce. Handle needs work but thrives in transition due to athleticism and finishing ability. Versatile defender with high upside on that end due to his size, length and athleticism. Improving handle and midrange game will be big as he continues to gain strength. Has NBA upside given shot-making ability with his size, length and athleticism.

Pure point guard with decent size and a +3 wingspan. Creative facilitator and ball handler who has a high basketball IQ. Impressive passer who uses craftiness to break down defense and get into the lane. Has a bit of a low release but is capable of hitting perimeter shots off catch. Better from midrange as shooter. Improving his shooting ability will be important. Improvement of his shooting will go a long way in determining ultimate success but projects as multi-year starter at high major level.

At 6-foot-9 with good athleticism, brings a lot of versatility to the floor. Not an elite shooter, but can definitely stretch the defense out to 22 feet. Better off the catch than off the dribble now, but he is more than a shooter. Beran has game off the dribble, and also proved to be a solid rebounder. Needs to continue to get stronger, but has a world of upside. Should be an excellent piece to the puzzle for Northwestern.

Diallo has fantastic physical tools to work with. He is right around 7-feet tall, has tremendous length, and can block shots. Right now his best ability is to run the floor and block shots from the weakside. Offensively he is a major work in progress, and needs time to refine his game - especially with his back to the basket. Diallo will provide some presence on the glass, though he struggles to root out defenders on the defensive end. Time will be needed, but in time the length and athleticism could play at a high level if his offense can develop even a little bit.

An extremely long and fairly athletic forward who can play either on the interior or on the wing. He isn’t the most skilled, but he is fairly comfortable attacking off the dribble, and then is a capable shooter off the catch from distance. He needs to continue to get stronger to compete at the high-major level, and tighten up his handle if he wants to be a full time wing. Must limit turnovers. On defense he provides versatility, and actually can be a shot blocker from the wing who comes down and helps against taller players. Should be a nice role player for the Huskers.

A bit of an undersized post. Flew under the radar for the majority of his high school career due to injury. Has good length. He is skilled inside of 10 feet, and shows some pick and pop potential from the center position. As a rebounder, Marble is good at controlling his area on the defensive end. Has a decent feel for where the ball is coming off, and getting to it on offense. Should be a major physical presence in college.

Late bloomer who is defensive specialist at this point. Decent size and length with improving athleticism. Has strong feel on defense and specializes as rim protector. Moves feet well and should be able to switch onto small defenders on perimeter. Raw offensively but most comfortable facing up from high post. Has improved footwork but can get better there. Budding back to basket and face up game. Passes well for position. Gaining strength and improving offensive skill will determine ultimate success. Has high major starter upside.

Strong lefty with smooth athleticism. Steady ball handler who can make some plays off the dribble. Slow release jumper, but has potential as an outside shooter off the catch. Has some improvisation to his game. Alert multi-positional defender. Will hit the boards. Has an edge to his game and intriguing potential.

Slender 6’6” frame. He’s lean, long and fluid. Bajema’s calling card is his ability to stroke jumpers from deep. He has good shot mechanics and preparation and is a shot maker off the off move and off the catch. Also showed an ability to deck the ball and make pull-ups and runners. The obvious area to improve here is strength and adding weight to his frame. He could also improve as a ball hander. There’s good upside here and with development could see him playing professionally because of his size and shot-making ability.

At 6-foot-5 and powerfully built, he is a beast going to the rim. Has tremendous athleticism to finish in traffic. While most players who are sons of coaches are praised for their basketball IQ, for him it is about athleticism and toughness on the wing. He needs to continue to improve his outside jumper, but the athletic tools are there for him to be a tremendous defender and someone who plays a good role on a successful team. Father is an assistant on staff at Auburn.

Hybrid forward with good size and decent length. Extremely versatile; will be able to play both three and four on both sides of the court. Passes very well for position; will be zone buster from high post. Can also get to the basket. Rebounds well on both ends of the court. Plus defender due to size, feel and motor. Improving perimeter shot and handle will be big for reaching upside. Versatility and intangibles make him project as ideal high major utility guy.

Not an explosive athlete but quite mobile with great hand-eye coordination. Needs to gain weight and improve strength. Catches and finishes well. Skilled passer with potential as a shooter. Effective in low and high post. Willing defender but could improve his defensive presence. Has intriguing upside and likely ends up as a first rounder.

A 7-footer with broad shoulders and long arms, he has all of the physical traits to be a high-level player. However, he has a long way to go with his offensive game. Needs to continue to get stronger and get more experience with his game. If the skills can catch up to the physical profile, he is someone who could project as a defense-first center who scores opportunistically.

Nn intriguing combo forward prospect with good size, very long arms and a projectable frame with wide shoulders. A very good athlete especially for his size. He is fluid, mobile and is pretty explosive off the ground. Offensively, Ihnen is a tad raw, but can make shots off the catch from 3, Is a willing and good passer, runs the floor in transition and has potential as a slasher with development of his handle as it is fairly loose at this stage. Defensively, he is loaded with upside due to his athleticism and physical gifts. He can already defend multiple positions as he breaks down and gives good effort on this end and moves well laterally.

Undersized combo guard who thrives in transition due to his athleticism and scoring ability. Solid driver who finishes well around the basket. Capable three-point shooter off the bounce. Has vision to create for others but best as a scorer. Improving player with strong frame who has potential as a defender due to instincts and lateral quickness. Can improve his playmaking ability and shot selection. Has chance to be role player with continued development.

A long athlete who is still growing. Has good athleticism and a natural feel for the game. Lack of strength needs to improve, especially to be effective long term on the defensive end. Terrific scorer who is effective at all three levels. Has a nose for the ball and the length to be a dangerous rebounder. Has tremendous upside, which makes him a potential lottery pick.

Versatile scoring wing with size to play multiple perimeter positions. Best asset is pure scoring ability. Can efficiently score from all three levels. Most comfortable with ball in his hands and can score pulling up or attacking basket. Strong handle for position. Has physical tools to be a plus rebounder. No physical limitations defensively although consistency and effort could improve there. Has shown significant strides throughout high school career. Should be good scorer in college with NBA upside if efficiency continues to improve.

Great size for such a highly skilled player. Smooth athlete. Has the frame to fill out nicely. Room for improvement in foot quickness. Has complete skill set with high basketball IQ and top-notch court vision as a passer. Has high release jumper with range. Loves to go left. Passes well off dribble. Quality rebounder. Can improve as a defender. Tremendous talent with upside. Trending towards lottery-pick status.

Jamal Mashburn Jr., the son and namesake of the former Kentucky star and NBA all-star, began his career with Richard Pitino at Minnesota and followed him to New Mexico, where he has been a three-year starter. Mashburn averaged 18.2 points per game as a sophomore and 19.1 as a junior, but saw that number drop to just 14.1 this year as a senior, as both his minutes and shooting percentages declined. While Mashburn saw slightly less volume this year, he was still a featured threat, ranking second overall on the team in both usage rate and percentage of total shots. And while inefficient overall, he was also still the most versatile scorer in the Lobos’ rotation and a true three-range threat. He attempted 28% of his total shots at the rim this year, but only made 42%, illustrating his struggles to finish in traffic with somewhat limited size and athleticism. Where he thrived, was when he was able to use his compact and sturdy frame to draw contact and get to the free-throw line, where he converted nearly 85%. His mid-range game, which was the biggest component of his arsenal as a junior with various pull-ups and step-backs, went from 49% of his total offense to 37%, and he made just 35% of those attempts. His three-point shooting dipped most of all, dropping from 39% to 33%. His turnover rate was also problematic this year, especially during league play when it jumped to over 12%. The hope going into the season was that with seemingly more weapons, Mashburn would have the benefit of not being the focus of opposing scouting reports. While that may have been true, the biggest take-away from the year is that he seems to be a player, and specifically a shooter, who needs significant volume to be at his best.

A high-level quarterback prospect as well. Has great size (length and strength) for a point guard. Plays powerfully in traffic. Loves to get to the rim, but can score from all three levels. Can distribute, but is a scorer first. High potential as a defender and rebounder. Can be prone to play with a high risk/reward ratio.

Very good size for a post with decent length and tremendous mobility. Extremely unique post prospect with legitimate perimeter skills. Plays with a high motor and has elite end-to-end speed for his size. Smooth stroke with range to three. Handles the ball very well for position. Shoot-first but has plus vision. Upside as rim protector and rebounder is elite. Could improve consistency, finishing with contact and shot selection while showing better commitment to defensive end. If everything comes together for him, he has a bright future in the NBA.

Limited height but tremendous quickness and body control. Strong with the basketball. Extremely ambidextrous and also an accurate one-hand passer with either hand. Scores from all three levels with a variety of shots. Tremendous distributor. Needs to tighten up his defense and also become a more physical defender.

Extremely quick athlete. Exceptionally fast and slippery with the basketball. A natural scorer from anywhere on the court. Can make tough shots. Also has the ability to create for others and distribute. Diminutive on defense but can be disruptive with his quickness.

A well-built, physical and explosive athlete. A well-rounded and versatile offensive player. Primarily a slasher who can both finish and find open teammates. Capable and improving outside shooter. Room for improvement as a full court ball handler. Quality rebounder for position and versatile defender. Should hear his name called in the first round of the NBA draft whenever he declares.

An advanced physical specimen. Powerfully built with some quick twitch explosiveness as a 6-6 wing. The lefty can get where he wants on the court and is a good playmaker. Manufactures buckets but has room to improve as an outside shooter. Is a great rebounder for his position. Scores on putbacks and can start the break. Imposing and versatile defender who should develop into an NBA draft selection.

Has all the qualities of a big-time post prospect. Length, athleticism, mobility, skill and basketball IQ are all in abundance. Finishes with touch and has nice footwork in the post. Threat to score out to 17-feet at this point. Expected to extend range. Rebounds outside his area. Conscientious defender. Expected to be a high lottery pick and possible No. 1 overall pick in draft. More Evaluations Evaluated 03/31/2020 Josh Gershon Scout national basketball recruiting analyst Read

Long and freakily athletic. Runs like the wind. Continues to physically fill out. Thrives in transition where he is a terrific finisher. Quick straight-line driver in half court. Jump shot progressing and has potential. Shot blocker with defensive versatility. Needs to tighten up his handle and improve his passing. Has tremendous upside that could place him in lottery at the draft.

A four-year player who started 108 of his 130 games at Duke, and was the team captain as both a junior and a senior, Jeremy Roach arrived in Durham after an equally decorated high school career. He was known as one of the best point guards in the class right away as a freshman, and ranked among the top 25 prospects in the national class from start to finish, despite an ACL injury that cost him his final year of grassroots basketball. At Duke, he evolved into more of a scoring guard, who played both on and off the ball. He’s had a knack for playing pick-and-roll since he was younger, and ranked in the 86th percentile as a ball-handler in that action as a senior, while also converting 53% around the rim as a lay-up maker per Pivot Analysis. The biggest area of growth in his game over the years has been his shooting. He made just 31% of his threes and 68% of his free-throw as a freshman, but improved in both categories with each passing year, finishing at 43% and 84% respectively this year. He also ranked in the 98th percentile in spot-up situations, showing his enhanced gravity when off the ball. Duke even began to run Roach off screens away from the ball, where his versatile scoring ability coupled by his playmaking made him dangerous. Defensively, he has a reputation for being adequate but sometimes inconsistent, although the metrics are much more favorable. Duke was nearly 12 points per possession better defensively when he was on the floor this season, per Pivot Analysis, as he made good progress on-the-ball, despite still having some lapses away from it.

Has a chisled physique and plays an active and physical game. Not an explosive leaper but a strong athlete with functional athleticism in traffic. A productive player with a solid all-around offensive game for a power forward. Currently a low 30 percent 3-point shooter. Didn't shoot a high free-throw percentage on Nike EYBL circuit. Aggressive and high quality rebounder. Picks up buckets with offensive rebounding. Is a versatile and tough defender with good feet.

Body continues to fill out. Quite skilled for a 7-footer. Still more of a finesse-type big man. More about skill than power. More a mobile athlete than explosive athlete. Has good touch and can stretch the defense to beyond the three-point line. Comfortable handling and passing the ball. Guarding ball screens could be a bit of an issue, but he should protect the rim well, and then provide both a roll man and a pick and pop guy on offense. Will block shots but has room to become more of a factor on defense. He needs to get stronger and develop a back to the basket game, but has significant ability on both ends of the floor. Should hear his name called in the second round of the NBA draft.

Has good length for a wing. Is a smooth athlete with body control, but not an explosive athlete. Versatile forward who can likely play big three and small ball four in college. Handles the basketball very well and has a good feel for the game. Can score in a variety of ways and is especially effective in the midrange. Is a solid rebounder. Playmaker off bounce who can score with jumper or facilitate for others with vision. Has range to three with jumper and can hit shots off catch and pull. Tends to overhandle but does have respectable ball skills for size. Just OK athletically and may be better suited to guard the four in college. Projects as high major starter; NBA upside will depend on improved athleticism and efficiency. Not the quickest defender but can utilize his length. Projects as a low first-round or early second-round draft pick.

Big body who can occupy space. Not an explosive athlete. Good basketball IQ and positional defender and rebounder. Effective in the high/low game. Knows how to seal his man and can also deliver a pass from the high post. Has reliable jump hook with his dominant left hand. Can make a face-up midrange shot. Should end up being a second-round draft choice.

Standing around 6-foot-3 and possessing good length, he has positional versatility on both ends of the floor. One of the better shooters from the guard position. Has good athleticism to complement that. Offensively he is best off the ball where he can be a catch-and-shoot guy that also attacks closeouts. On defense, he shows the ability to defend either guard spot physically and should project to be above-average on that end going forward.

Micah Peavy was a freshman starter at Texas Tech before transferring to TCU for the next three seasons. He played heavy rotation minutes, primarily coming off the bench as a sophomore and junior, before cracking the starting line-up as a senior and averaging 10.9 points, 4.9 rebounds, 2.6 assists, and 1.3 steals per game. TCU went to the NCAA tournament in all three seasons he was in the program. Peavy is defensive standout. He was TCU’s best on-ball defender and extremely versatile, showing switch-ability throughout the line-up. He plays with a super high motor, competes to the fullest on every possession, and has good wing size that would allow him to slide up the line-up and play some hybrid four (although that rarely happened this year with Emanuel Miller in that spot). Offensively, he’s at his best when he can get downhill to his dominant right side.
